title: sumbaviopsis albicans
intro: Sumbaviopsis albicans, also known as the white sumbaviopsis or poison tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is a small tree with milky white latex and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and acting as an analgesic. Sumbaviopsis albicans can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of conditions.
